old_claim.note: Safety Bicycle Design provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: Bicycles and tricycles provided the first commercial application context for Dunlop's pneumatic tire before later automobile use.

source_shape.source_locator: Britannica describes Dunlop constructing the pneumatic tire for his son's tricycle and says it was invented as an improvement on the bicycle before aiding automobiles.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source places Dunlop's practical tire in tricycle and bicycle use, then commercial production, before later automobile impact.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The corrected prerequisite captures the early vehicle market that made the tire useful without requiring the narrower safety-bicycle design node, whose own scope already includes pneumatic tires.

ontology_before: Pneumatic tire production depended on safety_bicycle_design, implying the specific safety-bicycle package enabled tire production even though that node already contains pneumatic tires.
ontology_after: Pneumatic tire production depends on the broader bicycle application context that drove early commercial use, while leaving safety-bicycle design as a neighboring transport design rather than a direct prerequisite.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: pneumatic_tire_mass_production now depends on bicycle and no longer directly depends on bicycle_safety_design.
would_reject_if: The node were later narrowed to tire supply for safety bicycles only rather than commercial pneumatic tire production.
A source showed early pneumatic tire commercialization required the Starley-style safety bicycle package rather than bicycle and tricycle use generally.
The bicycle node were rescoped away from human-powered two-wheel road vehicles.
short_reason: The source-backed application context is bicycle and tricycle use, not the narrower safety-bicycle package.
